name: Game Element
rule: A game element is any physical component of the game, a story passage, or a note the game instructed you to take.

name: Generic Tokens
rule: Use Generic tokens to keep track of anything that you want to; these were added for your convenience. Whenever there’s an effect that doesn’t use any of the specific tokens, you will be instructed to use a generic token to track it.

name: Random
rule: When you are asked to choose a random element, do the following based on the element:

name: Random Card
rule: Draw the top card from the indicated (facedown) deck.

name: Random Direction
rule: Draw the top card from the Minor Trauma deck. The arrow in the bottom left corner designates the randomly chosen direction. If it shows a rotating arrow, you may instead choose a direction. Place the drawn card at the bottom of the deck.

name: Random Element
rule: Roll one d10 for each element, lowest roll is selected.

name: Ignore
rule: “Ignored” elements or rules are not resolved. “Ignore” can be specific instances (accompanied by the word “this”) or constant.

name: Remove
rule: “Removed” elements are set aside and not used until instructed, or until the end of Battle if not.

name: Disable
rule: “Disabled” elements or rules are ignored until stated, or until the end of Battle if not.

name: Discard
rule: “Discarded” elements are Disabled and Removed.

name: Cannot
rule: The term “cannot” is absolute. It cannot be overridden unless explicitly instructed.

name: Gain
rule: Whenever you gain a game element, simply find it and add it to an appropriate place

name: Gaining Gear
rule: Gained Gear cards are added to the Argo Armory deck.

name: Gaining Gear in Battle
rule: If you gain a Gear card during a Battle, the Titan responsible for the gain may immediately equip that card. If the gained card has the Possession keyword, the Titan or Argonaut must immediately equip it.

name: Gaining Tech
rule: Gained Technology cards are added to the Technology deck.

name: Gaining Clues
rule: Gained Clue and Ally cards are placed next to your current Story and Doom cards.

name: Gaining Mnemos cards
rule: Gained Mnemos cards are placed in the play area of the Argonaut who gained them. If it is impossible for any Argonaut to gain a Mnemos card (III.5.5), instead add it to the Mnemos Vault (III.5.6).

name: Gaining Tokens
rule: Gained tokens are placed in the appropriate area.

name: Gaining Battle Tokens
rule: Tokens that have no effect outside of Battle (e.g. Ambrosia or Despair) are held by the Argonaut who gained them.

name: Gaining Other Game Elements
rule: Some game elements are not represented by physical components. If you gain any element that is not represented by a component, note it on either the Argo Sheet or an Argonaut Sheet, whichever is specified.

name: Loss
rule: When you lose a physical component, archive it unless instructed otherwise. When you lose a game element that is not represented by a physical component, erase it from the appropriate sheet (if it is singular) or lower its value by a specified number.

name: Gain/Loss Mitigation
rule: Some Technology abilities may allow you to ignore gaining or losing a specific game element (VIII.6.4.2d).

name: Voluntary Gain/Loss
rule: If the gain/loss is listed in the text of an active ability, story choice (IX.1.3), or any other non-forced effect, it is considered voluntary (a “payment”).

name: Unmitigable Loss
rule: Voluntary gain/loss cannot be mitigated or ignored in any way.

name: Gaining/Losing as a Group
rule: When you are instructed to “gain as a group” or “lose as a group", distribute the specified statistic or element among all of the Argonauts as you see fit; it does not have to be distributed evenly.

name: Archiving (Permanently Removing)
rule: When instructed to Permanently Remove a game element (or Archive it), place it back in the game box. It is no longer considered a part of the game state (though an effect may instruct you to return it).

name: Voluntary/Involuntary
rule: “Voluntary” effects are those which the Titan/Primordial has done with its own action/effect. “Involuntary” effects are any effects which a Titan/Primordial resolves but are not caused by itself.

name: Exhaust
rule: When instructed to exhaust a game element or ability, turn it 90 degrees or place a Generic Token on it to denote this. Cards remain exhausted until they are unexhausted by another effect.

name: Game State
rule: The "game state" refers to the collective set of conditions, variables, and rules that define the current situation and progress of the game being played. It encompasses all relevant information regarding the positions, statuses, resources, actions the players can take, and game elements within the game environment.

name: Effect
rule: An 'effect' is any text found on a game element, including the rulebook, that has the potential to alter the game state upon activation or meeting certain conditions. This includes symbols used as abbreviations for text. Effects are separated by periods ('.')

name: Active Effects
rule: An Active Effect is an effect that costs something to use. Active effects end once Resolved.

name: Constant Effects
rule: A Constant Effect is an effect with no cost to use. Constant effects must be resolved every time their timing condition is met and end once Resolved.

name: Passive Effects
rule: A Passive Effect is a constant effect with no timing conditions. Passive effects do not resolve and are instead continuous.

name: Resolving Effects
rule: To Resolve an effect, follow its instruction through to completion. This may require you to find those instructions in this rulebook (search for it in the Master Index). Afterwards, the effect is considered Resolved. Always resolve effects in the order you read them in (top to bottom, left to right). Always completely resolve one effect (and any effects that it triggers) before moving on to the next listed effect on a game element.

name: Triggers
rule: A “Trigger” is a condition that an effect can have. An effect with a trigger does not resolve unless its trigger’s condition is met. Any effect or ability with a specific trigger must be resolved after the trigger occurs or is checked, before resolving any effects other than the ones caused or enabled by it.

name: Modifier
rule: A “modifier” is any text on any game element that applies to an effect and changes how it resolves. A modifier can be permanent or temporary.

name: Immediate
rule: If an effect contains the word “immediately” it must be resolved the moment it is triggered and takes precedence over any other lingering effects as well as overruling any conflicting rules.

name: Instead
rule: Effects with the word “Instead” are resolved in place of another effect, which is Ignored (e.g. “Opening 2 instead” on a Gear card means that the value of its original “Opening” keyword is Ignored in favor of the new effect).

name: May
rule: When instructed to resolve an effect with the word “may”, you must choose whether or not to resolve the effect. Regardless of your choice, the effect is considered resolved after you make the decision. If an effect does not include the word “may”, you must resolve it.

name: When
rule: When instructed to resolve an effect with the word “when”, resolve this effect after you resolve the effect it refers to (or, in some cases, when you begin to resolve it), but before any other actions or effects. Example: If an effect triggers a “When you draw a Trauma card,” it activates immediately after you draw the card but before you resolve it.

name: When you are about to
rule: When instructed to resolve an effect with the phrase “when you are about to”, resolve it immediately before you resolve the effect that is about to happen (i.e. if an ability is triggered “when you are about to die,” it activates after another effect instructs you to die, but before it is actually resolved).

name: Timing-Conflict Resolution
rule: If more than one effect is triggered at the same time, you may choose the order in which these effects are resolved, unless stated otherwise. If any of these effects make another impossible or unviable, Ignore the impossible/unviable one. (Which effect is impossible/unviable could be different with different resolution orders.)

name: Owned Effects
rule: All effects in the game are “owned” by one of the following entities: an Argonaut, a Titan, the Primordial, the Argo, or the Game itself. An entity owns an effect if that effect is on any other game element that is exclusive to that entity.

name: Voluntary/Involuntary
rule: Effects are voluntary if the affected entity owns the effect. (I.2.13) Effects are involuntary if the affected entity does not own the effect.

name: Ability Box
rule: An ability box is any graphic container with effects inside on a Gear card, Titan sheet, Mnemos card, or Technology card.

name: Ability
rule: An ability is any effect within an Ability Box.

name: Ability Usage
rule: Abilities with no timing condition can only be used as a free action during their owner’s turn, all other abilities can only be used when their timing condition is met. Each available active (I.2.3.1) ability on a single game element can only be used once per Round.

name: Active Ability Costs
rule:
If any of the following are listed before an ability, you must resolve all of them in order to activate that ability. 
Fate/Rage/Danger. If any Triskelion stat is listed, you must gain the shown amount of that stat in order to activate the ability.
Exhaust. If the Exhaust symbol is listed, you must exhaust that ability box in order to activate the ability.
Discard. If the Discard symbol is listed, you must discard the game element the ability box is on in order to activate the ability.
Tokens. If a specific token symbol is listed, you must gain one of that token in order to activate the ability.

name: Named Abilities
rule: The text of some abilities is preceded by an ability name and, optionally, a subtype, preceding their name. These are called Named Abilities.

name: Keywords
rule: A keyword is a term used as shorthand for an ability used to convey specific common rules. All keywords are named abilities. See Appendix A for the list of all keywords and their definitions.

name: Stackable Keywords
rule: A Stackable Keyword is any Keyword followed by an ‘X’, unless stated otherwise in its definition. Multiple instances of a Stackable Keyword may be activated simultaneously.

name: D10s
rule: d10s are used for Attack Rolls, Evasion Rolls, Tests, and other miscellaneous Rolls. Treat the Progress symbol result as a ‘10’. A natural ‘10’ (on the die, no modifiers) on a d10 is always a success/hit whereas a natural ‘1’ is always a fail/miss. These dice are not piece limited.

name: Crit Die
rule: White d10s are called Crit Dice, and one of the dice in all Attack and Evasion rolls must be a Crit Die. Crit Dice have no significance outside of Attack and Evasion rolls.

name: Power Dice
rule: Power dice are used for Power Rolls and Armor Rolls. They come in three different colors (Red, Black, and White) and they are limited by the number of dice provided in the game box (X.5.7.1b). Power dice consist of three different symbols: Powers, Potentials, and Dots.

name: Rerolling Dice
rule: Many effects will allow you to re-roll some of your dice. For a single effect, each die can only be re-rolled once, and all dice being re-rolled must be declared before doing any re-rolls. If multiple players are rolling for the same effect at the same time, they must all declare whether they want to re-roll their dice before any of them does so.

name: Fate Re-rolls
rule:
You must gain 1 Fate for each die you decide to re-roll.
Using Fate, you can re-roll Attack Rolls, Evasion Rolls and Tests.
Using Fate, you cannot re-roll Power Rolls, Armor Rolls and any other roll not listed above.
To re-roll an Argo Test you must gain Argo Fate instead.

name: The Argo
rule: The Argo is represented by the Argo miniature that you move on the map. The Argo miniature is always on a Map tile.

name: Argo Sheet
rule: The Argo Sheet serves as the main means of tracking your campaign progress, resources, diplomacy, available Titans, and other campaign statistics. Whenever you gain or lose any of these statistics, adjust their value in the corresponding section of the Argo Sheet.

name: Crew
rule: Crew represents the strength of the able-bodied members of the Argo’s crew. Losing too much Crew will lead to a campaign loss.

name: Hull
rule: Hull represents the integrity of Argo’s hull. Losing too much Hull will lead to a campaign loss.

name: Argo Fate
rule: Argo Fate represents the collective destiny of the Argo and its crew. Like the Triskelion stat (III.3.3.2), the higher your Argo Fate, the more negative repercussions you’ll face. Gaining Argo Fate over 9 will lead to a campaign loss.

name: Argo Knowledge
rule: Argo Knowledge represents the collective knowledge about the Argo and the state of the world. It’s a cumulative stat that will only ever go up (IX.2.4). You cannot gain more than 20 Argo Knowledge in a Cycle.

name: Resources
rule: Resources are mainly gained from the Exploration deck and Primordial Battles. They are primarily used to craft Gear cards and activate certain Technology abilities. They differ between Cycles.

name: Mortal Resources
rule: Mortal resources are primarily gained from Exploration cards (VIII.3.2.1). They are noted in the Mortal part of the Cargo Hold section on the Argo Sheet.

name: Primordial Resources
rule: Primordial resources are primarily gained from Primordial Battles (X.6.3). They are noted in the Primordial part of the Cargo Hold section on the Argo Sheet.

name: Primordial Cores
rule: Of Primordial resources, Cores are the most valuable. You gain Cores from critically wounded BP III cards. When you gain a Core, write its Primordial’s name and update its quantity in the Cargo Hold under Cores.

name: Divine Resources
rule: Divine resources represent extremely rare items that are gained when instructed. They are noted in the Hidden Hold section of the Argo Sheet.

name: Rare Resources
rule: Rare resources include all resources (other than Cores) not listed on the Argo Sheet. They are incredibly limited. When you gain a Rare resource, write both its name and quantity under Rare Resources.

name: Priests
rule: Priests have no inherent effects, but some Technology abilities may have them as a cost.

name: Sisyphus Tears
rule: Sisyphus Tears are used to replay Battles (X.6.1.1).

name: Pygmalion Stones
rule: Pygmalion Stones are used to save titans who died in Battle (X.6.4.1). Ignore this rule until instructed otherwise (Cycle II inward odyssey): Whenever an Argo-bred Titan dies, make a Pygmalion mark. When you have 3 Pygmalion marks, gain a Pygmalion Stone.

name: Echoes of Recollection
rule: When an Argonaut dies, before resolving any other effects, you may spend an Echo of Recollection to ignore that Argonaut death. This cannot be used to “save” an Argonaut from a Mnemos Breakthrough.

name: Diplomacy Score
rule: Factions are represented by your Diplomacy Score and Diplomacy Modifier with that Faction, and many game elements will refer to those values.

name: Diplomacy Track
rule: The Diplomacy track on the Argo sheet is for noting your current standing with each Faction in the Cycle. Whenever you gain or lose Diplomacy with a Faction, mark the appropriate space on the corresponding track. The numbers in parentheses above the track sections denote the Diplomacy Modifier you should apply if you are within a given threshold. Whenever you test Diplomacy, modify your result by that value.

name: Diplomacy Triggers
rule: Various game elements will include Diplomacy triggers (I.2.5) that refer to your Diplomacy Threshold or Score with the Local Faction (VIII.1.5).

name: At War
rule: If you ever reach the At War section of any Faction’s Diplomacy track, ignore Diplomacy gain and loss with that Faction.

name: Events
rule: Events are anything written on the Timeline.

name: Adding Events
rule: When instructed to add an Event to the Timeline, note its name on the indicated day.

name: Types of Events
rule:
The type of Event determines when during a Campaign Round it is resolved.
Battle Events. Battle events trigger during the Encounter Step.
Advancement Events. Advancement events trigger during the Advancement Step.
Story Events. Story events trigger during the Story Step.

name: Progress
rule: For each Progress you gain, you must place 1 Progress token on your current Story card or the Inward Odyssey card (IX.2.4), if possible.

name: Story Cards
rule:
Story cards are how you win Aeon Trespass: Odyssey. After completing a Cycle’s story introduction, you will gain Story card 1 on side A (Story Card 1A).
Main story progression is resolved when you fulfill all requirements noted on your current Story card. This is frequently finding a specific map tile or placing a certain number of Progress tokens on it.
When you meet the current Story card’s requirements, you will resolve its story progression during the Story Step (VIII.7). 
Every Cycle has 4 Story cards. Completing Story card 4 (A or B) is the only way to win a Cycle.

name: Doom
rule: For each Doom you gain, you must place 1 Doom token on your current Doom card.

name: Unavoidable Doom
rule: Unavoidable Doom is Doom that cannot be mitigated or lowered in any way.

name: Doom Cards
rule:
Doom cards show the slowly unfolding plans of the villain (or villains) of the Cycle and how they come to fruition. After completing a Cycle’s story introduction, you will gain Doom card 1 on side A (Doom Card 1A).
Doom progression is resolved only if you fulfill all the requirements noted on your current Doom card. This is always a certain number of Doom tokens.
When you meet the current Doom card’s requirements, you will resolve its doom progression during the Doom Step (VIII.8).
Every Cycle has 3 Doom cards.

name: Winning a cycle
rule: You only win if a Story Paragraph explicitly says so. Completing Story card 4 (A or B) is the only way to win a Cycle.

name: Losing a cycle
rule:
There are many ways to lose. When you reach one of the conditions listed below, the game will direct you to a story paragraph that informs you how to proceed. If you are not directed to a specific paragraph, instead see Campaign Loss in your current Storybook.
Resolving the final Doom card (3B) of the Cycle.
Crew or Hull reaches 0
Gaining Argo Fate over 9.
Getting to the end of the Timeline.
Initiating a Battle with 0 Titans aboard the Argo.
Other narrative-related events.

name: The Argonauts
rule: There are always exactly 4 active Argonauts, no matter how many players take part in a campaign. If you play with a smaller group, distribute the Argonauts between players as desired.

name: Least and Most Likely Argonaut.
rule:
The “least likely Argonaut” is the Argonaut with the least amount of Mnemos cards. In case of a tie, the one with the least amount of Mnemos nodes. If there’s still a tie, choose randomly from the tied players. Conversely, the “most likely Argonaut” (or the ‘strongest’ Argonaut) is the Argonaut with the most Mnemos cards and Mnemos nodes. If there’s a tie, choose randomly from the tied players.
"Fated Mnemos" cards unintuitively do not count as "Mnemos Cards".

name: Argonaut Sheet
rule: An Argonaut Sheet, just like the Argo Sheet, is used to track progress, but for a given Argonaut. Here, you will write an Argonaut’s name (from the Argonaut Portrait card or one devised by you) and keep track of the many aspects of the Argonaut that the sheet is tied to.

name: Skills
rule: Each Argonaut has six Skills: Courage, Cunning, Endurance, Fury, Will and Wisdom. They have no inherent meaning and are just used for Tests.

name: Skill Value
rule: Whenever you need to know the value of a particular Skill for an Argonaut, look at their portrait, Mnemos cards, Fated Mnemos cards, and notes section of their Argonaut sheet and add up all of the values for that skill shown in those places (all positive values except for Fated Mnemoses). For convenience, you may write the just calculated value in the Skills section of the Argonaut sheet to skip this process in the future, but make sure to update the Argonaut sheet if any of that Argonaut’s Mnemos cards, Fated Mnemos cards, or notes change.

name: Skill Modifiers
rule: Any Rolls made with a Skill have the value of that Skill added to it. (Skills can be negative.)

name: Triskelion
rule:
The Triskelion is a triple dial that represents the union of Mortal, Divine, and Titan elements that make the junction between Argonauts and Titans possible, shown through the stats of Danger, Fate, and Rage, respectively.
Stats are shared between the Argonaut and the Titan they choose to take into Battle. 
Any Danger, Fate, or Rage gained in an adventure stays until instructed to lose it.

name: Danger
rule:
Danger is gained when you are attacked and also through suffering other negative effects. Many abilities and Gear cards become more powerful when you reach a certain Danger value.
If you gain Danger over 9 during a Battle, perform a Trauma Draw (VI.9.6), even if it wasn’t dealt during a Primordial Attack.

name: Fate
rule:
Fate is gained to re-roll your dice and to pay for many abilities. High Fate may enable additional negative effects from Primordial Attacks.
If you gain Fate over 9 during a Battle, immediately draw a Moiros card (X.7.12). Unless the Death by Fate Trait is in effect.

name: Rage
rule:
Rage is gained each time you attack, giving you access to additional abilities from your Kratos Table. 
If you gain Rage over 9 during a Battle, your Titan dies at the end of its current action. If it is not in the middle of one of its own actions, it dies immediately.

name: Over 9 Out of Battle
rule: If any of your Triskelion stats goes over 9 outside of Battle, your Argonaut dies (III.4.2).

name: Leaving the Argo
rule: A story effect may cause an Argonaut to leave the Argo (retire). When this happens, take a new Argonaut Sheet, draw a new Portrait card (or choose a Skill to apply a +1 bonus to) and draw a random Mnemos card from the Mnemos Vault.

name: Argonaut Death
rule:
Your Argonaut dies only when explicitly stated.
When an Argonaut dies, immediately consult the Rude Awakening Special Event of your current Cycle, it will instruct you on how to create a new Argonaut. 
Never discard or destroy the Argonaut Sheets of deceased Argonauts, as some events might reference them later.

name: Gaining Mnemoses
rule: When you gain a Mnemos card, find the associated Mnemos card in the game box and write its title on your Argonaut Sheet. Then, write the paragraph number of the first Mnemos Breakthrough above the Breakthrough symbol on the corresponding Mnemos track.

name: Mnemos Cards
rule:
On the back of a Mnemos card you will find a title and an illustration, as well as a cryptic note as to its nature. 
On its front, the card shows the Cycle number, three Mnemos levels, each with a corresponding ability and a Breakthrough paragraph number, as well as Skill bonuses for your Argonaut and the Mnemos traits. 
You gain access to the Level 1 ability as soon as you gain the Mnemos card, while the Level 2 and Level 3 abilities are unlocked only after resolving the first and second Mnemos Breakthroughs, respectively. 
The Skill bonuses listed at the bottom of the card are applied onto your Argonaut Sheet upon gaining the Mnemos card. They are permanent, as long as you have the Mnemos card. 
The 3 words at the top of a Mnemos card are its traits.

name: Mnemos Nodes
rule: Mnemos nodes are like experience points for your individual Mnemos cards. Each Mnemos progress track goes from 0 to 10 nodes. A Mnemos Breakthrough is triggered whenever you reach 3, 7, or 10 Mnemos Nodes on a single Mnemos card. See also Gaining Mnemos Nodes (IX.2.2.4).

name: Mnemos Breakthrough
rule: When a Mnemos card’s Breakthrough is triggered, during the Story Step, resolve the Mnemos adventure code (IX.2.5) (written above the Breakthrough symbol on the Mnemos progress track) and then unlock the next Mnemos Ability.

name: Mnemos Card Limit
rule: Each Argonaut can have a maximum of 2 Mnemos cards. If an argonaut is instructed to gain a Mnemos card and is already at their limit, the Mnemos card goes to another random Argonaut instead. If all Argonauts already have 2 Mnemos cards, the new Mnemos is instead added to the Mnemos Vault.

name: Mnemos Vault
rule:
The Mnemos Vault is a deck that consists of all Mnemos cards you have unlocked during your campaign, which are not currently assigned to any Argonaut. Keep it separate from other Mnemos cards.
When an Argonaut dies or leaves the Argo for any reason, return their unresolved Mnemos cards to the Vault.

name: Resolved Mnemoses
rule: Once you resolve the last Breakthrough of a Mnemos card, it becomes resolved. This means that your Argonaut leaves the Argo or stays with a consequence. If they retire, this Mnemos does not return to the Mnemos Vault. Instead, Archive it.

name: Katharsis
rule: Any Mnemos resolution which tells you to mark a Choice Matrix node is called a Katharsis. When you choose a Katharsis, write the Matrix code in the Katharsis box on your Argonaut Sheet.

name: Fated Mnemos
rule: Fated Mnemos cards, though they have a similar namesake, are not considered Mnemos cards for the purposes of any effect.

name: Fated Mnemos Card Limit
rule: Each Argonaut can have 2 Fated Mnemos cards at the same time. These do not count towards the limit of 2 Mnemos cards per Argonaut.

name: Gaining Fated Mnemoses
rule: When you gain a Fated Mnemos, search the game box for all of the Fated Mnemos cards associated with the Cycle you are currently playing, shuffle them and draw a random card. Then, note it on your Argonaut Sheet similar to a regular Mnemos (III.5.1). Fated Mnemos cards are always gained black side up.

name: Resolving Fated Mnemoses
rule: If all the nodes to the left of the first breakthrough symbol are marked, resolve it during the Story Step. This simply involves flipping the card over, writing the blue side (trauma growth) ability on your Argonaut Sheet, then returning the card to the box. (Fated Mnemoses do not have associated adventures.)

name: Trauma Abilities
rule: There is no limit to the amount of trauma growth abilities a single Argonaut can have. (You can even gain the same trauma growth ability more than once and their effects will stack.)

name: Afflictions
rule: Afflictions introduce additional effects that are similar to Conditions (X.7.11) but are tied to the Argonauts.

name: Boons
rule: Boons, though rarer, are the positive equivalent of Afflictions. These can come in the form of personal traits, equipment or allies. Note these on your Argonaut Sheets.

name: Mazetouched
rule: No inherent effects.

name: Consumed by Rage
rule: A Titan with the Consumed by Rage affliction must apply all Fate-gated Primordial effects, regardless of their Fate value.

name: Black Breath
rule: A Titan with the Black Breath affliction has -1 Ambrosia Limit (X.7.14.1).

name: Fractured
rule: A Titan with the Fractured affliction must apply all Fate-gated Primordial effects, regardless of their Fate value. In addition, they cannot gain nodes for Fated Mnemoses.

name: Moirai Mistake
rule: A Titan with the Moirai Mistake affliction has “Crit Miss: Immediately full miss the Attack.”

name: Gaia-born
rule: All Dreamwalker-class Titans may also be referred to as Gaia-born Titans.

name: Argo-bred
rule:
All Titan classes besides Dreamwalker are collectively referred to as Argo-bred Titans.
You can have more than one Titan of a given Argo-bred class.
You cannot breed (VIII.6.7.1a) a Titan of a given Argo-bred class if you already have one, but you can still gain it from other sources, such as adventures or fusing (VIII.6.7.2a).
You can only take one Titan of a given Argo-bred class into Battle, regardless of the number of Titans of that class you have.

name: Titan Sheet
rule: Each Titan is represented by a large Titan Sheet that includes all the basic information about a given Titan class. Besides the Titan class name and illustration, it shows the Titan Power die, Speed value, inherent abilities, Trauma Table, and Kratos Table.

name: Titan Power
rule: Power die you include in every Power Roll you perform. Some Titans have Keywords next to their Power die that apply to all of their Attacks.

name: Titan Speed
rule: A value used for determining the distance a Titan’s Movement Action can go (X.4.3).

name: Ability Boxes
rule: Abilities available to you while you are junctioned with this Titan.

name: Trauma Table
rule: The Trauma Table features the Titan’s Trauma thresholds (Minor, Major, Grave, and Obol), which are value ranges that correspond to your current severity level. When you are instructed to check your Trauma Table, compare your Danger value to the thresholds and see which one it falls within.

name: Kratos Table
rule:
The Kratos Table shows Rage levels and corresponding abilities and bonuses applied to every Attack that Titan performs.
You must apply the benefits of your current Rage level, as well as all preceding levels, on a Titan’s Kratos table during each of its Titan Attacks.

name: Kratos Table Abilities
rule:
Similar to constant abilities, Kratos Table abilities do not have a cost, but they are resolved differently. Most Kratos Table abilities modify the Titan’s Attack, by giving you access to either additional keywords or unique effects.
Most Kratos Table levels give you a choice of two options. These are always exclusive: If you decide to apply one of these options, you cannot apply the other. Options may include more than one effect. Kratos Table symbols that are linked with a “+” symbol are both part of the same option.
You do not have to use any of your Kratos Table abilities until you reach the second ability window, at which point you must activate all applicable effects from your Kratos Table (1 option per level) that are still available.

name: Effects That Ask for Named Titans
rule: Some effects may require you to note a Titan’s name somewhere (like the Timeline). In such a case, if the Titan is not named, you must immediately name it as part of resolving this effect.

name: Pattern Cards
rule:
Patterns will override a Titan’s Trauma Table or Kratos Table and sometimes introduce new rules or abilities.
Whenever a Titan is assigned a Pattern, they must get a name. 
A Titan can have more than one Pattern, but if both Patterns relate to the same game element, the newer one will override the older (and that card will be discarded).
At the start of Cycle I, Patterns are unique, one-of-a-kind things: a Titan is assigned a Pattern from a Story effect and it becomes its permanent feature (it cannot be removed). When a Titan with an assigned Pattern dies, that Pattern is archived. 
Once you unlock the Gymnasion deck in Cycle II, gain Pattern cards you archived up until that point and put them in the deck.

name: Spartan and Delphian Dreamwalker
rule: Spartan and Delphian strain pattern cards are cycle-specific base modifiers to dreamwalker-class titans that are applied by default in their respective cycles. These patterns cannot be assigned otherwise and never go to the Gymnasion deck.

name: Weapons
rule:
Weapons are any Gear cards that occupy Hand slots. Weapons can occupy one, two, or three Hand slots. Weapons are the only source of Offensive Gear Stats, though many also have Defensive Gear Stats.
By default, Titans have two Hand slots.

name: Armor
rule:
Armor cards usually grant one or more Defensive Gear Stats.
By default, Titans have one Armor slot.

name: Support
rule:
Support cards infrequently have Defensive or Offensive Gear Stats, but will give you access to unique abilities and effects.
By default, Titans have two Support slots.

name: Attachments
rule:
Attachment cards must be attached to another game element (such as other Gear cards or the Titan Sheet). They may modify the game element they are attached to, apply additional effects, or give you access to completely new abilities. 
By default, a Titan Sheet can have up to three Attachments and each other Gear card can have up to one. 
If a game element is Exhausted, Discarded, or Lost, its attachments are as well.

name: Gear Attributes
rule: I don't think this is something that needs to be looked up on the regular. This is your bread and butter fot this game

name: Sun Resistance X
rule: Each Sun Attack hit deals X less Danger to you.

name: Midas Resistance X
rule: X additional Midas tokens may be placed on this card before it becomes aurified (see X.X.X midas tokens).

name: Weapon Traits
rule:
Weapon traits include: Axe, Blade, Bow, Cestus, Club, Discus, Hammer, Javelin, Khopesh, Knife, Shield, Slabsword, Spear, Sword, Whip, Kusarigama, Lance, Scythe, Pistol, Lightningthrower, and Harpoon.
If a weapon has multiple weapon traits, when you declare it as an active weapon (X.5.1), choose one of its weapon traits and treat it as if it only had that weapon trait for the duration of that Titan Attack Sequence.
Treat weapon cards with no weapon traits as having the Other weapon trait.

name: Fists
rule:
If, at any point of the Battle, a Titan loses their weapon card(s), they may immediately equip a Fists card and use it as a One- or Two-Handed weapon (abiding by Slot limitations). For all intents and purposes, Fists are treated as any other Gear card.
Each Titan can use a single Fists card. If a Titan has a Fists card and loses it, they cannot equip another one that Battle.
If, at any point of the Battle, a Titan gains a weapon card that uses up their hand slot(s) occupied by Fists, you must set the Fists card aside. It is then no longer equipped on the Titan.

name: Exhausted Gear
rule: Exhausted Gear cannot be used as an active weapon (X.5.1), used for its defensive statistics (V.2.2), nor can its active abilities be used.

name: Exhaustible Gear
rule: Some game effects refer to Exhaustible Gear. Exhaustible Gear includes all Gear cards with the Cumbersome keyword or with active abilities that include Exhausting in their cost.

name: Gear Backside
rule: The back of Gear cards includes information about which Cycle the Gear card belongs to, the Technology card that allows you to craft the Gear, as well as some flavor text. Some Gear cards may also include additional rules there and other Gear cards are double-sided, with different names on either side.

name: Ascendancy
rule:
When you use a Gear card with a Power Level that is lower than the number of the current Cycle, that Gear card suffers all of the following penalties:
If the Gear card is a weapon, it cannot utilize nor place any Kratos tokens.
The Gear card cannot benefit from Power Reroll X.
Increase the Fate cost of all abilities on the Gear card by 1 (or 2 in Cycle 3). (If there is no Fate cost initially, this does not add one.)
If the Gear card is Armor or a Shield, during Armor rolls, ignore 1 rolled Potential.
The Gear card has -1 to its listed Evasion Rerolls.
The Gear card has -1 to its listed Evasion Bonus.

name: Regular Primordials
rule: There are usually two regular Primordials in each Cycle, as noted on the Evolution Track on the Argo Sheet. You can expect to face these at regular intervals (VIII.5.2). As the campaign progresses, they will evolve and become more powerful (VI.2.2).

name: Adversary
rule: Adversaries are special Primordials that hunt you on the Voyage Map (see VI.13 for more).

name: Boss Primordial
rule: Boss Battles may be triggered from various Story and Doom adventures. Each Cycle leads to a final showdown with the boss Primordial.

name: Primordial Facing
rule: Every Primordial miniature has a front, which is the direction it is facing. Which side of the Primordial is the front is up to you, it’s just important to stick with whatever choice you make.

name: In Front
rule: To find out if a game element is in front of the Primordial, draw an imaginary line passing along the edges of the spaces occupied by the Primordial miniature’s front. All spaces beyond this line in the direction the Primordial is facing are considered to be in front. Adjacency is not required to be considered in front.

name: In Rear
rule: Rear designates only the spaces adjacent to the Primordial which are on the opposite side of the model from the front. These are marked with the Rear space symbol on the Primordial diagram.

name: Primordial Levels
rule: Primordials have a Level which is most often tied to its Evolution or Battle Track during campaign play, and to the Tree of Knowledge for the Mnestis Theatre. A Primordial’s Level determines its Attribute values and any Traits it may have.

name: Level Boxes
rule: Each Primordial sheet has a list of 9 Level Boxes. A Level Box holds all the information regarding a particular Primordial Level, which includes the Level numeral, Main Attributes Bonuses, a list of added or removed Traits, and, sometimes, a definition of a newly introduced Trait.

name: Level Attribute Bonuses
rule:
The following is a list of Attribute Bonuses from Level Boxes. These do not stack with previous bonuses.
AT bonus. Increase the AT of each BP card by this value.
Danger bonus. Increase the Danger dealt by each Primordial Attack Sequence (or each hit, if stated as such) by this value. Only increase the Danger of hits that already deal Danger.
Evasion dice bonus. Increase the number of Evasion dice that need to be rolled for each Primordial Attack Sequence by this value.
Escalations. At the start of Battle, Escalate the Primordial a number of times equal to this value.
Danger/Fate Bonus. Increase the Fate dealt by each Primordial Attack Sequence (or each hit, if stated as such) by this value. If the Attack Sequence does not deal Fate, increase the Danger instead. Only increase the Danger/Fate of hits that already deal Danger/Fate (respectively).

name: Evolution and Battle Tracks
rule: Most Battles will tell you to either mark one Evolution box or one Battle box on the respective Primordial’s track. The numeral in the box you mark is the Level used for that Primordial fight. (Adversaries and Bosses have no numbers because they are always considered Level 1.)

name: Unavoidable Knockback
rule: Resolved the same as Knockback X (A.48), but it cannot be avoided or modified in any way. When a Titan suffers Unavoidable Knockback, they receive it in a direction that makes them go immediately away from the Primordial, without going through it. If there are 2 or more equally viable directions, you may choose which to receive. This is triggered every time a Primordial ends its movement on a Titan (VI.8.4). The amount of Knockback is listed on the Primordial sheet.

name: Blindspot
rule: Blindspots are spaces the Primordial cannot see, shown on the Primordial Diagram. Titans in Blindspots are excluded from targeting, unless the targeting specifically mentions Blindspots. Titans in the Blindspot gain +1 Precision.

name: Weakspots
rule: Weakspots don’t have any inherent effects of their own, but other effects may cause them to gain effects.

name: Lumbering
rule: A Primordial with Lumbering cannot suffer Pushback or Knockback.

name: Signature and Routine
rule: You should already know what these are about.

name: AI Cards
rule: You should already know what these are about.

name: Window of Opportunity
rule: AI Cards, as well as BP, Signature, Routine, and any other Primordial game elements, may have the Window of Opportunity symbol on them in front of any effect. These are a time frame in which any number of Titan Reactions can be used. During a single window of opportunity, each Titan may use any number of Reaction abilities in any order. All Reactions must happen before the effect on the game element with the symbol.

name: Resolving an Attack
rule:
To resolve a Primordial Attack, perform the following steps in order.
Step 1: Targeting. Read the lines in the “targeting” area of the card one by one from top to bottom until you find the first one that you can resolve (VI.7.2). Once you find one that can be resolved, resolve it and do not check further targeting lines. 
If no target line can be resolved and the card is an AI card, discard the card (Skip all remaining steps of VI.7.1) and the Primordial performs its Routine. 
Whatever game element is selected becomes a Target. A Titan that becomes a Target in the Targeting step remains the Target until the end of this Attack.
Step 3: Primordial Attack. Resolve a Primordial Attack Action (VI.9) or a Primordial Judge Action (VI.10), whichever is instructed.
Step 4: After Attack. Resolve After Attack effects, if any (VI.7.3).

name: Priority Target
rule: The Titan with the Priority Target token (X.1.12) is considered to be the Priority Target and is a tiebreaker for Targeting. If two or more Titans are ever equally viable Targets (fulfill all instructions of the same targeting line) but one of them is the Priority Target, the Priority Target becomes the Target.

name: After Attack
rule:
Always resolve the After Attack effects unless the Target performed a Chain Break or died (X.7.3).
After Attack instructions are resolved against each Target.
If a Primordial Attack includes an After Final Attack instruction, the After Final Attack instead only takes effect after resolving all Attack Sequences queued by that Attack.

name: Primordial Movement
rule:
Always Move the Primordial along the shortest possible path to the Target of the AI card.
If the Primordial is not in a straight line from its Target, Move it in such a way that it zig-zags until it is in a straight line from the Target, then continue the Move.
Always move the Primordial one space at a time to resolve any triggered effects.
At the beginning of a move, the Primordial always turns to face the Target first.
Primordial movement is not hindered by any other game element.
The Primordial stops its Move when in range (for Ranged Attack Actions), when adjacent (for any other Attack Actions), or when it has moved a number of spaces equal to its Speed.

name: Primordial move onto Destructible Terrain
rule: When a Primordial moves onto a space containing a Destructible Terrain tile, that tile is Destroyed and removed from the board.

name: Primordial move through Titan spaces
rule: When a Primordial moves through a space containing a Titan, that Titan suffers Crash.

name: Primordial steps on Titan spaces
rule: When a Primordial ends movement on a space containing a Titan, that Titan suffers Crash and Unavoidable Knockback (VI.3.1.1).

name: Appear Behind
rule: Jump, but the Primordial has Unlimited Speed and moves to the furthest space adjacent to the Target.

name: Appear in Front
rule: Jump, but the Primordial has Unlimited Speed and moves to the closest space adjacent to the Target.

name: Primordial Jump
rule: Move as normal, but ignore Crash for passed Titans (VI.8.3).

name: Land on Top
rule: Jump, but the Primordial has Unlimited Speed and moves to the closest space occupied by the Target. The Target (only) is Displaced and does not suffer Crash.

name: Move Lateral
rule: Check the four cardinal directions from the Primordial for the shortest straight line that would end with the Target(s) of the Attack Action in a straight line from the Primordial. Move the Primordial that way.

name: Move to LOCATION
rule: The Primordial has Unlimited Speed for this movement, and moves to LOCATION instead of towards the Target(s)

name: Move X
rule: Move as normal, but use X instead of the Primordial’s Speed value.

name: Primordial Attack Action
rule:
Step 1: Move. If there is a Move instruction, resolve a Primordial Move Action.
Step 2: Check Range. Check if the Target(s) are in range of the Attack Action (adjacent by default). If they are not, stop resolving the Attack Action. (Proceed to After Attack.)
Out of Reach (OoR) Evade. If a Target was in range before Step 1 but no longer is due to a Titan’s Reaction, that Reaction is referred to as an Out of Reach Evade (or OoR for short).
Step 3: Initiate Attack Sequences. Queue (X.7.22) an Attack Sequence against each Target. Queue all Attack Sequences from a single Attack as a Set (X.7.22.1a).

name: Primordial Attack Sequence
rule:
Step 1: Turn. Turn the Primordial towards its Target, unless the Target is a Zone.
Step 2: Evasion Roll. In the upper right corner of the card that triggered this Attack Sequence, there is a number of Evasion dice and an Evasion Roll difficulty number. The Target rolls the specified number of dice. 
If the Target has any Evasion rerolls (or wants to reroll with Fate), they may use them before counting hits.
Step 3: Count Hits. Add any Evasion bonus the Target may have to each Evasion die rolled, then the Target receives a number of hits equal to the number of die results that didn’t meet or beat the Evasion roll difficulty.
Step 4: Attack Consequences & Effects. If a Titan is left with at least one hit, it must resolve all effects of the Attack Action.

name: Crit Evade
rule: If a Titan rolls a natural ‘10’ with the Crit Die (I.4.1.1) on the Evasion Roll, they resolve all “Crit Evade:” effects.

name: Crit Evade Fail
rule: For each time a Titan rolls a natural ‘1’ on the Evasion Roll, they resolve all “Crit Evade Fail:” effects.

name: Full Evade
rule: If there are zero hits, it is a Full Evade. Trigger all “Full Evade:” abilities and skip Step 4 of this Attack Sequence.

name: Gain Danger From Attacks
rule: When instructed to gain Danger from an Attack, you must first immediately perform an Armor Roll if you have any non-exhausted cards with Armor dice. Afterwards, gain Danger equal to value dealt minus the number of Potentials rolled in the Armor Roll. If a Titan gains at least 1 Danger this way, it immediately performs a Trauma Draw (VI.9.6).

name: Armor Roll
rule: To perform an Armor Roll, roll a pool of Power dice equal to the total of all Armor dice on all of your equipped cards (Remember, if you use a weapon to defend, you cannot use other equipped weapons during the same Attack).

name: Massive Danger
rule:
When you would gain Danger that would put you over 9, raise Danger to 9 and treat the following Trauma Draw as if it was increased to the actual value you would've been at. If your Danger temporarily goes up to at least 14, 18, or 21 this way, add the respective Obol cards to the Obol deck immediately. Cards added to the Obol deck as a result of Massive Danger remain there until the end of Battle.
Danger 14+. Add the “You died horribly” card to the Obol deck. 
Danger 18+. Also add the “Your gruesome death becomes legend” card to the Obol deck. 
Danger 21+. Also add the “Your death transcends reality” card to the Obol deck.

name: Raising a Trauma Level
rule: When instructed to raise your Trauma Level by X, gain enough Danger to be in the next Trauma Threshold (as noted by your Trauma Table) and repeat X times. Treat the Massive Danger levels as higher Trauma Thresholds as necessary. If an Attack instructed you to do this, perform a Trauma Draw.

name: Trauma Cards
rule:
There are four severity levels of Trauma cards: Minor, Major, Grave, and Obol, the same as the thresholds found in your Trauma Table. 
When you discard a Trauma card, place it at the bottom of its corresponding deck. 
All Trauma cards are either positive or negative, as denoted by the +/- sign in the bottom right corner of the card. You Live! Obols are positive traumas, and You Died. Obols are negative.

name: Obols (and Obol Draw)
rule: To Perform an Obol Draw, shuffle the Obol deck and draw the top card. Resolved and discarded Obols are always shuffled back into the Obol deck rather than being placed on the bottom. (An Obol Draw is still a Trauma Draw.)

name: Simultaneous Obol Draws
rule: If you are ever forced to perform two or more Obol Draws at the same time, you die instead. (Consecutive effects are not simultaneous.)

name: Surviving a Trauma Draw
rule: A Titan “survives” a Trauma draw if they did not draw a Trauma card that instructed them to die.

name: Laser Attack
rule: To perform a Laser Attack, draw a line between the Primordial and the Target. If this line crosses over a Titan or Obstacle Terrain tile that is adjacent to the Target, that Titan/Terrain tile immediately becomes the Target. If a Terrain tile becomes the Target this way, it is destroyed and the rest of the Attack Action is skipped.

name: Double/Triple Laser Attack
rule: Resolved as a Simultaneous Attack consisting of one Laser Attack originating from every front space. If any of these Lasers does not connect with a Target, the first crossed Obstacle Terrain becomes a Target.

name: Sideways Laser Attack
rule: When a Laser Attack instructs you to “continue sideways”, then, after hitting a Black Ice Terrain tile, resolve additional Laser Attacks (as Double/Triple Laser Attack) from the two sides of the tile orthogonal to the side hit. This Attack does not destroy Black Ice tiles.

name: Omnilaser Attack
rule: Resolved as a Double/Triple Laser Attack, but the Lasers originate from every side of the Primordial.

name: Timefront Laser Attack
rule: Resolve a Laser Attack originating from every Timefront space.

name: Range Attack X
rule: Ranged Attacks can be performed against a Titan as long as it is within X spaces of the Primordial, instead of requiring adjacency.

name: Sequential Attack
rule: Sequential Attacks are performed against multiple Targets instead of one. Each Attack only gets queued if the previous one wasn’t ended by a Chain Break (X.7.6). Primordial Movement during this Attack does not cause crash.

name: Simultaneous Attack
rule: Simultaneous Attacks are performed against multiple Targets instead of one. Queue Attacks as a set (X.7.22).

name: Sun Attack
rule:
Target every space that is not shaded.
Shaded: Every space adjacent to a non-shaded Titan, Obstacle Terrain tile, or Obscuring Terrain tile that is opposite the Sun Edge is shaded.
Sun Edge: The Sun Edge is the Board Edge opposite the Primordial’s Front.

name: Zone Attack
rule:
Zone Attacks are performed against a set of spaces instead of Titans; Queue (as a set) Attack Sequences against each Titan in the Zone.
Zone X: Performed against all spaces within X spaces of the source of the Attack, including diagonally.
Diagram Zones: Performed against the specific spaces shown on the diagram.

name: Judge Test
rule:
A Judge Test is considered a Solo Argonaut Test (IX.1.6.2a) for all purposes. To resolve a Judge Test, perform an Argonaut Test. In the upper right corner of the card that Triggered the Judge Action, there is a number of dice and the Test difficulty. The Target rolls the specified number of dice instead of just one. Each Fail is instead considered a Hit.
 If the Target is left with at least one hit, they must trigger all effects of the Judge Action. If these include Danger, resolve Danger from Attacks (VI.9.3) as normal, but skip the Armor Roll step.

name: BP Cards
rule: You know 'em, you love 'em

name: Creating VPs
rule:
Until instructed to “create a VP” from an effect, the VP is ignored.
When a Primordial VP is created, remove the interchangeable part of the Primordial miniature and switch it with the Vantage Point part. Alternatively, if you don’t want to switch the parts, you can use the VP space marked on the Primordial Sheet instead. (Not all VPs have interchangeable parts.)
If a Primordial has no associated VP description, you cannot create VPs on it by any means.

name: Climbing a VP
rule: A Titan adjacent to a Primordial with a VP may perform a Climb test as a free action (X.7.20). If you succeed, place your Titan on the VP space. Only one Titan can occupy a single VP at a given time.

name: Falling down from a VP
rule: At the end of the Primordial Round, most VPs force you to perform a Hold on test (X.7.20) to find out if you fall down. The particular effects of falling from a VP are explained on the Primordial Sheet.

name: Movement and VPs
rule: A Titan may move from a Primordial VP space to a space adjacent to the Primordial using a Movement Action, or any move-like ability, as if they were adjacent spaces. If a Titan suffers Knockdown or involuntary movement while on a VP space, they must first be Displaced to a space adjacent to the Primordial.

name: VPs, Adjacency, and Range
rule: Titans on VPs are considered adjacent and in range 1 to the Primordial and all Titans adjacent to this Primordial. They measure range to other Titans and game elements from the space occupied by the Primordial that is closest to that Titan or game element. Other Titans adjacent to the Primordial are not considered to be adjacent to Titans on VPs.

name: Introducing an Adversary
rule: The Storybooks will instruct you on when to introduce an Adversary into your campaign. When an Adversary is introduced into a campaign, write its name on the Adversary Track.

name: Activating the Adversary
rule:
Whenever you are instructed to resolve an Adversary symbol, resolve one of the following, checking each in order (if you resolve one of them, skip checking the rest).
If the Adversary is already present on the Map, move it (VI.13.6)
If the Adversary is not on the Map and one symbol is drawn during the Exploration Step (only), nothing happens.
If the Adversary is not on the Map, and you either drew two symbols during this Exploration Step or triggered one symbol during any other Step, spawn the Adversary: Place the small miniature of the current Adversary four tiles away from the Argo. If you can’t place it four tiles away, place it as far away as possible.
If you spawned the Adversary during the Exploration Step, ignore all other Adversary Activation symbols until the end of the Exploration Step.

name: Adversary Battles
rule: When told to resolve an Adversary battle, go to the corresponding Battle Scenario in the Storybook. Adversaries are not tied to a single Cycle and you may need to refer to other Cycles’ Storybooks.

name: Argo Move Onto Adversary
rule: An Adversary Battle is only triggered from Adversary movement (never Argo movement).

name: Adversary Movement Rules
rule:
The Adversary always moves one tile towards the Argo along the shortest available path. 
If Adversary movement causes it to end on the same Map tile as the Argo, remove its miniature from the map and trigger an Adversary Battle. If the Adversary is already on the same tile as the Argo during an activation, it does not move but you still trigger the Adversary Battle.
The Adversary moves one tile per symbol.
The Adversary can only move through revealed tiles.
The Adversary can move onto any adjacent Map tile, even if it is not connected by a directional arrow. It ignores tile sectors and all Map symbols.
If there is more than one equally viable path, the Adversary prioritizes horizontal movement as long as it brings it closer to the Argo. “Closer”, in this case, means along the shortest possible path of revealed map tiles (do not count distance of facedown/unplaced tiles). 
 In Cycle 2, instead, prioritize vertical movement.

name: Gameplay Flow
rule: This game is played over many sessions. During a game session, you will likely play a few Campaign Rounds. Each Campaign Round is made up of a series of Steps. Each of these Steps incorporates one or more of the game’s Phases: Voyage Phase, Adventure Phase, or Battle Phase.

name: Gameplay Round
rule: A Campaign Round is one in-game day on the Timeline.

name: Steps
rule: Steps make up the structure of a Campaign Round and are always resolved in a fixed order.

name: Phases
rule: Phases are gameplay segments that differentiate which rules are in effect at any moment.

name: Phase Types
rule: There are three different Phases in ATO: Voyage, Battle, and Adventure.

name: Phase Rule of Thumb
rule: You are always in a game Phase. If you’re not in the Adventure Phase or the Battle Phase, you’re in the Voyage Phase.

name: Phase Entering
rule: When you are in a Battle, you are in the Battle Phase. When you are reading passages in the Storybook, you’re in the Adventure Phase. Always return to the Voyage Phase after finishing a Battle or Story section.

name: Phase Campaign Round
rule: Any Campaign Round Step can consist of any combination of Phases.

name: Campaign Round Sequence
rule: Each Campaign Round, you will go through the Campaign Round Steps (VIII.1-10), resolving them in order.

name: Events
rule: All Campaign Round Steps always happen, but there are not always events during a Step in a given Campaign Round. Steps VIII.1-3 always have events.

name: Always Move
rule: During the Movement Step, you must move the Argo to an adjacent Map tile.

name: Map Arrows
rule: You must choose a direction from the arrows on your tile. You cannot move in a direction without arrows. If there is more than one arrow in the same direction, you must pick one before revealing the new tile, the tile in that direction will be Sectored.

name: Factions on Map Arrows
rule: Faction symbols on Map Arrows tell you which Faction(s) are on the Map tiles your current tile connects to.

name: Map Arrows Lock Symbols
rule: If a map arrow has a padlock symbol on it, ignore that arrow unless you have the required Technology.

name: River Lock Symbol
rule: (Cycle 1 only) Ignore Map Arrows with this symbol unless you have the XXX Technology card.

name: First Cataract Lock Symbol
rule: (Cycle 2 only) Ignore Map Arrows with this symbol unless you have the XXX Technology card.

name: One-Way Canals Lock Symbol
rule: (Cycle 2 only) These Map Arrow symbols represent directions in which you are free to move one way, but are Locked from the other.

name: Second Cataract Lock Symbol
rule: (Cycle 2 only) Ignore Map Arrows with this symbol unless you have the XXX Technology card.

name: Sluice Gate Lock Symbol
rule: (Cycle 2 only) Ignore Map Arrows with this symbol unless you have the XXX Technology card.

name: Placing Map Tiles
rule: If the tile you’re moving to isn’t revealed, find the Map tile with the number of the arrow selected on its back and place it adjacent to the Map tile you are traveling from, so that the directional arrows on the shared border of the two tiles align. Make sure to orient the new tile in the same way as all of the other tiles. Place a reminder token of some sort on the tile to mark it as Unexplored.

name: Ambrosia (Terrain Trait)
rule: When a Titan ends its movement on this tile (voluntarily or not), it gains 1 Ambrosia token. If a Titan starts its turn on this tile and doesn’t move until the end of this turn, it gains 1 Ambrosia token. If a Titan moves through this tile but does not end its movement on it, test (6+). If you fail, gain 1 Ambrosia token.

name: Board Edge
rule: This tile counts as a Board Edge (X.1.6).

name: Boundless
rule: You cannot voluntarily move onto this tile. If you would be forced to move onto or beyond this tile or Board Edge, you die.

name: Chasm
rule: Titans cannot voluntarily move through Chasm spaces. If a Titan ends involuntary movement on a Chasm space, it dies. If it is pushed back or pulled through a Chasm space, it dies. If a Chasm tile is placed under a Titan, it dies.

name: Cover
rule: Titans on this tile have +1 Evasion .

name: Destructible
rule: When the Primordial moves onto this tile or a Titan is involuntarily moved onto it, the tile is destroyed.

name: Reinforced Destructible
rule: When this tile would be destroyed, instead flip it and the corresponding terrain card over. It then becomes Destructible.

name: Elevated
rule: No inherent effect.

name: Exposed
rule: If the Primordial is on this tile, it has -1 To-Hit.

name: Indestructible
rule: This Terrain tile cannot be destroyed in any way. It invalidates Terrain placement on top of it.

name: Inhabited
rule: Apply any bonuses and penalties listed on the Terrain card based on your Diplomacy Score with the Local Faction. If this Terrain tile is destroyed, lose 1 Diplomacy with the Local Faction.

name: Obscuring
rule: This tile blocks Line of Sight except to or from Targets standing on any of its spaces.

name: Obstacle
rule: Titans cannot move through this tile.

name: Slippery
rule: When a Titan voluntarily moves onto this tile, it must continue to move in the same cardinal direction for the rest of the movement as long as it is on this tile or until it cannot move further. If a Titan moved diagonally onto this tile, it must continue in a cardinal direction instead.

name: Trap
rule: This tile has special interactions with the Primordial, listed on the Terrain card.

name: Treasure
rule: This tile has special interactions with Titans, listed on the Terrain card.

name: Ambrosia Limit X/+X
rule: Your Ambrosia Limit becomes X or is increased by X, respectively. If you have more than one instance of the former, then the one with the greatest X is used (before applying modifiers).

name: Armor-Piercing
rule: During the Power Roll, ignore Hardened.

name: Armor Reroll X
rule: During the Armor Roll step of a Primordial Attack Sequence, you may reroll up to X Armor dice.

name: Ascended
rule: This Gear ignores Power Level penalties.

name: Assist X
rule: During another Titan’s Power Roll, if, after applying all bonuses, their total Power is less than the AT value and you are within your weapon’s range, you may gain 1 Rage and a Combat Fatigue to immediately add X Break tokens to the Kratos Pool. This ability can only be used if it allows the Attacker to Wound.

name: Attack Reroll X
rule: During an Attack Roll, you may re-roll up to X Attack dice with no cost.

name: Auto-Black X
rule: During the first ability window, place X Black tokens (X.1.9.6) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Auto-Break X
rule: During the first ability window, place X Break tokens (X.1.9.1) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Awakening Lock
rule: You cannot Awaken.

name: Black X
rule: During the second ability window, place X Black tokens (X.1.9.6) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Bleeding X
rule: Gain the Bleeding Condition card (unless you already have it) and place X Bleeding tokens on it.

name: Bleeding Limit X/+X
rule: Your Bleeding Limit becomes X or is increased by X, respectively. If you have more than one instance of the former, then the one with the greatest X is used (before applying modifiers).

name: Block X
rule: After counting hits from an Evasion Roll, subtract X hits.

name: Break X
rule: During the second ability window, place X Break tokens (X.1.9.1) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Burden X
rule: During the First or Second Ability Window, take X Condition, Despair, or Ambrosia tokens from the nearest Titan. (This does not count as gaining those tokens.)

name: Burn
rule: Gain 1 Danger for each Metal Gear card you have equipped and discard all Flammable Gear cards. If this is part of a Primordial Attack Sequence, add the Danger gained to the Danger gained from hits.

name: Bypass
rule: You may move through other Titans. You cannot end your voluntary movement on a space occupied by another Titan.

name: Carving X
rule: When you wound the Primordial, test Wisdom (8+). On a success, you immediately gain X additional non-Core Primordial resources from that BP card. These resources are not multiplied by the Primordial level.

name: Clutch
rule: During the Power Roll step, if, after applying bonuses, you have less total Power than the AT value by exactly 1, you may gain 1 Power. After you finish resolving this Attack Sequence, discard your active weapon.

name: Combo-Breaker: X spaces
rule: If you are not the Priority Target, you may move X spaces and exhaust a Gear card with a Weapon Trait matching the current space on the Combo Track to break the Combo. This ability is not stackable.

name: Self Combo-Breaker: X spaces
rule: Identical to Combo-Breaker, but you can only use the Gear card which this ability is on to break the Combo (you do not have to exhaust an additional card). This Gear must still possess a matching Weapon Trait.

name: Commit
rule: When this weapon becomes your active weapon (X.5.1), gain 1 Fate.

name: Consumed
rule: Treat “consumed” as an Instant Death effect.

name: Crash
rule: Gain 1 Danger. Knockdown.

name: Cumbersome
rule: During the second ability window, if you successfully wounded with this weapon, exhaust it. Treat this as a voluntary exhaust.

name: Daze
rule: During the first ability window, if you are adjacent to the Primordial, you may turn the Primordial so that you are in a Rear space.

name: Deadly
rule: During an Attack Roll, replace one of your regular Attack dice with an additional Crit die. If you roll only one Attack die, you gain a Crit Chance on a natural 9–10 result instead.

name: Death X
rule: Gain the Death Condition card (unless you already have it) and place X Condition tokens on it. If you already have the Death Condition card and X is lower than the number of Condition tokens you have, discard Condition tokens until you X matches.

name: Displace
rule: Move to an empty space adjacent to its current position. If a Titan is displaced and there is no space it can be legally displaced to, it dies instead.

name: Diversion
rule: During the second ability window, place a Diversion token (X.1.9.3) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Dodge
rule: When you are about to resolve an Evasion Roll, gain +1 Evasion for this roll.

name: Doomed
rule: During this effect, dice cannot be re-rolled using Fate.

name: Elation X
rule: During the second ability window, you may discard X Despair tokens or Ambrosia tokens to place X Opening tokens in the Kratos Pool (X.1.9.2).

name: Escalate X
rule: Escalate X times (X.5.10.6). Do not look at the removed cards.

name: Evolving
rule: When you equip a Gear card with this keyword, write the Gear’s name on your Argonaut sheet in the Mnemos section. The Gear instructs you on what you need to do to evolve it. Track your progress on doing so by marking nodes on the Mnemos track. If this Gear card is equipped by another Argonaut, continue keeping track on the original sheet. If the Argonaut the original sheet belongs to dies, transfer the progress to another living Argonaut’s sheet. If you permanently lose the Gear card, erase it from the sheet; you lose all evolution progress on it.

name: Fate Armor
rule: This card’s Armor Dice may also be used to mitigate Fate dealt by Primordial Attacks. If you are dealt Fate and Danger, you may distribute the mitigation between the two.

name: Fire X
rule: During the second ability window, place X Fire tokens (X.1.9.4) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Frontlines
rule: If your Titan is dead, at the End of Battle roll a d10. On a ‘1’, your Argonaut dies as well.

name: Glaciate
rule: To Glaciate a space, place a Black Iceberg Terrain tile there.

name: Giant Glaciate
rule: To Giant Glaciate a space, place a Giant Black Iceberg Terrain tile there.

name: Greater Pass X
rule: During the Clear the Kratos Pool step, choose up to X Kratos tokens to remain in the pool.

name: Hardened
rule: A BP card with this keyword has +2 AT. Ignore this ability if you have a Crit Chance.

name: Heal
rule: Take the top card from the Wound Stack and remove it from the Battle. It does not provide resources at the end of the Battle.

name: Heartseeker
rule: During the Draw BP card step of a Titan Attack Sequence (X.5.6), you may look at the top two BP cards and choose which one to attack. Shuffle the other card back into the deck.

name: Hide
rule: If you are not in front of the Primordial, you may activate this ability at the end of your turn to become Hidden. If you have the Priority Target token, pass it to the Titan with the highest Rage other than you.

name: Hidden
rule: As long as you are Hidden, you have +1 Evasion and +1 Precision. You stop being Hidden if you move in front of the Primordial, gain the Priority Target token, after the second ability window of your Attack, or at the end of your next turn. (Place a generic token on the game element granting you Hide as a reminder.)

name: Hope X
rule: During the second ability window, place X Hope tokens (X.1.9.7) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Incinerated
rule: Treat “incinerated” as an Instant Death effect.

name: Inspire X
rule: During the first ability window, choose up to X Knocked Down Titans, they may immediately stand up.

name: Jump
rule: At the start of movement, you may activate this ability to ignore up to 1 space of a Terrain tile.

name: Advanced Jump
rule: Ignore up to 2 spaces instead.

name: Knockback X
rule: Move the affected miniature X spaces directly away from the source of Knockback in a straight line (or in a specific direction if specified).

name: Knockback From Attacks
rule: If Knockback is caused by a Primordial Attack, it affects the Target(s) only if the Target(s) are within the Attack’s range.

name: Knockback From Responses
rule: If Knockback is caused by a Primordial Response, it affects the Attacker only if they are within adjacency.

name: Knocked Back Titans
rule: Knocked back Titans ignore all Terrain tiles they move through except Obstacles. If a Titan would be forced to move through a Destructible Obstacle Terrain tile, stop its movement, remove the Terrain tile from the Battle Board and place the Titan in its space, then the Titan suffers Crash. If a Titan would be forced to move through an Indestructible Obstacle, it stops on an adjacent space right before the Obstacle, then suffers Crash.

name: Knocked Back Primordials
rule: Knocked Back Primordials can move through any Terrain tiles and miniatures without hindrance. They destroy any Terrain tiles (except Indestructible) they move through and cause Crash and Unavoidable Knockback to Titans as normal.

name: Knockback Into Board Edge
rule: If the affected miniature is Knocked Back into a Board Edge, it continues to move along the edge, if possible, away from the source of Knockback.

name: Knockdown
rule: Gain the Knockdown Condition card, “Falling down” side up, and tip your miniature on its side. Follow the rules of the Condition card.

name: Knockback From Attacks
rule: If Knockdown is caused by an Attack, it affects the Target only if it is within the Attack’s range.

name: Knockback From Responses
rule: If Knockdown is caused by a Response, it affects the Attacker only if they are within adjacency.

name: Laser Resistance X
rule: Each Laser Attack hit deals X less Danger to you. In Cycle IV+, this loses its Keyword status and becomes a Defensive Statistic instead.

name: Lifeline
rule: When you are about to die from a Chasm space, Displace instead. Alternatively, when you are about to die from falling from a Boundless Board Edge, stop adjacent to it. This ability can be used even if Knocked Down or otherwise prohibited from using active abilities.

name: Lumbering
rule: Cannot be involuntarily moved.

name: Masterwork
rule: During an Attack Roll, you may reroll 1s with no cost.

name: Midas Immune
rule: ?, no effect yet

name: Motivate X
rule: During the second ability window, choose another Titan. That Titan may move up to X spaces. (Reminder: If you gain multiple instances of a Keyword, they do not have to stack and can therefore be resolved separately.)

name: Opening X
rule: During the second ability window, place X Opening tokens in the Kratos Pool (X.1.9.2).

name: Overbreak X
rule: During the second ability window, if your Total Power exceeds the AT value by at least 1, place X Break tokens in the Kratos Pool (X.1.9.1).

name: Pass X
rule: During the Clear the Kratos Pool step, choose up to X Opening and/or Break tokens to remain in the Kratos Pool.

name: Perishable
rule: After you finish resolving a Titan Attack Sequence with this weapon, discard it.

name: Precise
rule: During the Draw a BP card step (X.5.6), after drawing the card you may choose to draw the top card of the BP discard instead. If you do, place the first drawn card back into the BP deck and shuffle it.

name: Provoke
rule: During the first ability window, gain the Priority Target token and turn the Primordial to face you.

name: Power Reroll X
rule: During the Power Roll step of an Attack, you may re-roll up to X Power dice.

name: Pull X
rule: Move the affected miniature X spaces towards the source of the Pull, along the shortest possible path.

name: Pull over other Miniatures
rule: The affected miniature can move through spaces occupied by other miniatures.

name: Pull over Terrain
rule: The affected miniature must resolve the effects of any Terrain tiles it moves through.

name: Pull over Destructible Obstacles
rule: If a Titan is about to be pulled into a Destructible Obstacle (e.g. a Column), remove the Terrain tile from the Battle Board, the Titan suffers Crash, then continue the Pull.

name: Pull Over Indestructible Obstacles
rule: If a Titan is about to be pulled into an Indestructible Obstacle, it suffers Crash and continues to move along the Obstacle’s edge along the shortest possible path towards the Primordial.

name: Adjacency
rule: If the affected miniature is or becomes adjacent to the source, the Pull does not end. Instead, move the source directly away from the affected miniature the remaining number of spaces, then finish the Pull. The source’s movement is voluntary, the affected miniature’s movement is involuntary.

name: Titan Timing
rule: Titans may use the Pull X keyword during either the first or second ability window.

name: Pushback X
rule: Turn the source towards the affected miniature. Then, move X spaces in the direction of the affected miniature, pushing it in a straight line.

name: Pushback over other Miniatures
rule: Pushed Titans can move through spaces occupied by other miniatures. If a Titan ends involuntary movement on a space with another miniature, the other miniature is Displaced.

name: Pushback over Terrain
rule: The affected miniature must resolve the effects of any Terrain tiles it moves through.

name: Pushback if not Adjacent
rule: If the affected miniature is not adjacent, move the source in its direction along the shortest possible path.

name: Pushback over Destructible Obstacles
rule: If a Titan is about to be pushed into a Destructible Obstacle, remove that Terrain tile from the Battle Board, the Titan suffers Crash, then continue Pushback.

name: Pushback over Indestructible Obstacles
rule: If a Titan is about to be pushed into an Indestructible Obstacle, displace it to an adjacent space that would allow the Primordial to continue movement onto a space previously occupied by the Titan, then continue Pushback. If the Titan dies from displacement, then once the Primordial has moved into the space formerly occupied by the Titan, the Pushback stops.

name: Pushback over Board Edge
rule: If a Titan or Primordial is about to be pushed into the Board Edge, it is displaced to an adjacent space in a way that would allow the source of Pushback to move onto a space previously occupied by the affected miniature, then continue Pushback.

name: Pushback and Titan Timing
rule: Titans may resolve the Pushback X keyword during either the first or second ability window of their Attack or when specifically instructed to resolve it immediately.

name: Quantum
rule: For the purposes of Rapid Adaptation, if attacking from the Blindspot, your weapon can have any Weapon Trait

name: Ranged Y–X
rule: To attack with this weapon, you must be at least Y and up to X spaces away from your target. This keyword is not stackable.

name: Reach X
rule: You may attack from up to X spaces away. This keyword is not stackable.

name: Reflex
rule: Move up to 1 space.

name: Advanced Reflex
rule: Move up to 2 spaces.

name: Superior Reflex
rule: Move up to 3 spaces.

name: Reposition X
rule: During the second ability window, you may move up to X spaces.

name: Reinforce X
rule: When you are about to resolve an Armor Roll, add X red dice to your Armor dice pool.

name: Advanced Reinforce X
rule: When you are about to resolve an Armor Roll, add X black dice to your Armor dice pool.

name: Superior Reinforce X
rule: When you are about to resolve an Armor Roll, add X white dice to your Armor dice pool.

name: Rewind
rule: Place your Titan on your Time Anchor. If your Time Anchor is not on the Board, you are erased from existence instead.

name: Rocksteady
rule: When you are about to suffer Knockdown, you may use this ability to ignore it.

name: Rouse X
rule: During the second ability window, place X Rouse tokens (X.1.9.6) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Rollout
rule: When you are about to suffer Knockdown while adjacent to the Primordial, suffer Knockback 4 instead.

name: Rush
rule: Move with +1 Speed and perform a Melee/Reach Attack with Auto-break 1. You must move at least 3 spaces and reach your target via the shortest possible path.

name: Improved Rush
rule: Move with +2 Speed and perform a Melee/Reach Attack with Auto-break 1 and +1 Precision. You must move at least 3 spaces and reach your target via the shortest possible path.

name: Sacrifice
rule: During the first ability window, you may gain 1 Danger to place one Break token in the Kratos Pool (X.1.9.1).

name: Scale
rule: When you are about to perform a test to climb a Vantage Point or Elevated Terrain, automatically succeed at that test instead.

name: Second Chance
rule: After you draw a Trauma or Obol card, you may ignore its effect and discard it. Draw and resolve another card from the same deck (note: a discarded Obol is immediately shuffled back into the Obol deck). You cannot ignore the second draw in any way. This ability can be used even when knocked down or otherwise prohibited from using active abilities.

name: Shaded
rule: Until the start of the next Titan Round, treat your Titan as being on as shaded space.

name: Solace
rule: Discard a Mind Condition card or a Despair token from yourself or an adjacent Titan.

name: Spiral X
rule: During the Attack Roll step, you may reroll up to X hits to lose 1 Fate for each. If you reroll a Crit die this way, the original result does not grant you a Crit Chance.

name: Spotlight
rule: During the first ability window, if you have the Priority Target token, gain 1 Red Power Die for this Attack.

name: Stalwart
rule: When you are about to suffer Knockback, you may use this ability to ignore it.

name: Succor
rule: During another Titan’s Power Roll, if, after applying all bonuses, they have less total Power than the AT value by exactly 1 and you are within your weapon range of the Primordial, you may gain 1 Rage, a Combat Fatigue, and discard your active weapon to add 1 Power to that Power Roll.

name: Suppress
rule: Shuffle the discard pile denoted by the subtype into the appropriate deck.

name: BP Suppress
rule: Shuffle the BP discard pile into the BP deck.

name: AI Suppress
rule: Shuffle the AI discard pile into the AI deck.

name: Temporal  Reflex
rule: When you are about to suffer Crash, ignore it. If you would suffer Unavoidable Knockback, displace instead.

name: Time-Clocked
rule: Gain the Time-Clocked Condition card, Departing side up and remove your miniature from the board. Follow the rules of the Condition card.

name: Tireless
rule: When you are about to activate the Cumbersome keyword, you may ignore it.

name: Titan Possession
rule: Once you equip this Gear card on a Titan, you have to equip it on this Titan for every subsequent Battle. You may, however, junction this Titan with a different Argonaut.

name: Argonaut Possession
rule: Once you equip this Gear card on an Argonaut, you have to equip it on that Argonaut’s Titan for every subsequent Battle.

name: Transform
rule: Use this ability to flip this Gear card to the other side. This is always an active ability.

name: Trick
rule: Weapons with the Transform keyword and Trick may immediately flip to the other side when you meet the Trick condition.

name: Trauma Trick
rule: You may immediately flip this card after you resolve a Trauma card.

name: Wound Trick
